tweet.text:
Crypto stocks inch up post news about compromise on key provision in crypto bill
"The final rewards text in the CLARITY Act is now public," said Coinbase Global's (COIN) chief policy officer, Faryar ​Shirzad, in an X post.

Cryptocurrency-linked stocks were trading higher during pre-market trading on Monday post the news that a deal had been reached on a key provision in the crypto bill.

Coinbase Global (COIN): +2.27% to $195.60; Circle Internet Group (CRCL): +4.81% to $104.50; Strategy (MSTR): +2.08% to $180.85; IREN (IREN): +2.28% to $46.70; MARA Holdings (MARA): +1.83% to $11.67; Riot Platforms (RIOT): +1.51% to $18.78.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) was 0.31% higher at ~$78.80K, Ethereum (ETH-USD) was up 0.61% to $2,336.81, and Dogecoin (DOGE-USD) was 2.13% higher at $0.11.

The bill stalled after banks opposed a provision that would allow stablecoin issuers and crypto firms to offer yield-bearing products and other incentives that could draw deposits away from traditional banks, potentially making it harder for them to fund lending.

In March, key senators and the White House are said to have reached a tentative agreement on the language to be included in the cryptocurrency legislation, which aims to resolve differences between banks and digital asset firms over stablecoin yield.

What was included in the agreement was unclear and not guaranteed to win support from banking and crypto industries.

On May 1, Punchbowl News reported that Senator Thomas Tillis and Angela Alsobrooks finalized a compromise to restrict stablecoin yield and rewards, with the Senate looking toward a crypto markup this month.

Punchbowl News reportedly obtained the text of that agreement.

"In the end, the banks were able to get more restrictions on rewards, but we protected what matters—the ability for Americans to earn rewards, based on real usage of crypto platforms and networks," said Shirzad in his social media post.

"Now that this issue is behind us, it's time to focus on the broader bill," he said.

"While this debate has been underway, lots of progress has been made on other areas like token classification, DeFi, and tokenization. We're excited to review the full, final text, and for the bill to move forward," said Shirzad.

"It's time to get CLARITY done," he noted.
